mappings.rationaleAI: The electromagnetic spectrum from radio waves through gamma rays, Maxwell's theory, and Hertz's discovery of radio waves directly address electromagnetic waves (B) and their properties across the spectrum (B.2).

The photon, photoelectric effect, wave-particle duality, and laser applications address the quantum theory of light (C.8), photon phenomena (C.8.a), and sources of coherent electromagnetic waves (B.4).

The history and science of optics covering wave theory, diffraction, interference, polarization, lenses, telescopes, microscopes, and fibre optics comprehensively addresses light waves (C) and focusing/imaging (D).

Maxwell's prediction of electromagnetic waves, his equations unifying electricity, magnetism, and light, and his influence on radio development address electromagnetic wave properties (B.1), radio waves (B.2.a), and light wave properties via Maxwell's equations (C.6).

The invention of radio from Maxwell's theory through Hertz's experiments and Marconi's wireless telegraphy directly addresses radio waves (B.2.a), sources of radio waves (B.3.a), and transmission of electromagnetic waves (B.5).

Pythagoras's harmonic ratios, Fourier analysis of sound waves, and mathematical structures in music directly address physical aspects of musical sound (E.6) including pitch, timbre, and overtones (E.6.a).
Echolocation using sound reflection, the physics of returning echoes, and comparison with sonar technology address sound waves' nature and properties (E.1) and reception of sound (E.4).
The speed of light from special relativity, time dilation, and length contraction address the velocity of light and its measurement (C.2).
Infrared and submillimeter astronomy studying cold objects in space through infrared telescopes and space observatories addresses infrared radiation (B.2.c) and blackbody radiation sources (B.3.c.i).
Einstein's photoelectric effect paper establishing light's particle nature directly addresses observed photon phenomena (C.8.a).
